NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. Set to “The Lilies.” A Psalm of David. Save me, O God! For the waters have come up to my neck.
NKJV.2: I sink in deep mire, Where there is no standing; I have come into deep waters, Where the floods overflow me.
NKJV.3: I am weary with my crying; My throat is dry;  My eyes fail while I wait for my God.
NKJV.4: Those who hate me without a cause Are more than the hairs of my head; They are mighty who would destroy me, Being my enemies wrongfully; Though I have stolen nothing, I still must restore it.
NKJV.5: O God, You know my foolishness; And my sins are not hidden from You.
NKJV.6: Let not those who wait for You, O Lord GOD of hosts, be ashamed because of me; Let not those who seek You be confounded because of me, O God of Israel.
NKJV.7: Because for Your sake I have borne reproach; Shame has covered my face.
NKJV.8: I have become a stranger to my brothers, And an alien to my mother’s children;
NKJV.9: Because zeal for Your house has eaten me up,  And the reproaches of those who reproach You have fallen on me.
NKJV.10: When I wept and chastened my soul with fasting, That became my reproach.
NKJV.11: I also made sackcloth my garment; I became a byword to them.
NKJV.12: Those who sit in the gate speak against me, And I am the song of the drunkards.
NKJV.13: But as for me, my prayer is to You, O LORD, in the acceptable time; O God, in the multitude of Your mercy, Hear me in the truth of Your salvation.
NKJV.14: Deliver me out of the mire, And let me not sink; Let me be delivered from those who hate me, And out of the deep waters.
NKJV.15: Let not the floodwater overflow me, Nor let the deep swallow me up; And let not the pit shut its mouth on me.
NKJV.16: Hear me, O LORD, for Your lovingkindness is good; Turn to me according to the multitude of Your tender mercies.
NKJV.17: And do not hide Your face from Your servant, For I am in trouble; Hear me speedily.
NKJV.18: Draw near to my soul, and redeem it; Deliver me because of my enemies.
NKJV.19: You know my reproach, my shame, and my dishonor; My adversaries are all before You.
NKJV.20: Reproach has broken my heart, And I am full of heaviness; I looked for someone to take pity, but there was none; And for comforters, but I found none.
NKJV.21: They also gave me gall for my food,  And for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
NKJV.22: Let their table become a snare before them, And their well-being a trap.
NKJV.23: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they do not see; And make their loins shake continually.
NKJV.24: Pour out Your indignation upon them, And let Your wrathful anger take hold of them.
NKJV.25: Let their dwelling place be desolate; Let no one live in their tents.
NKJV.26: For they persecute the ones You have struck, And talk of the grief of those You have wounded.
NKJV.27: Add iniquity to their iniquity,  And let them not come into Your righteousness.
NKJV.28: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living,  And not be written with the righteous.
NKJV.29: But I am poor and sorrowful; Let Your salvation, O God, set me up on high.
NKJV.30: I will praise the name of God with a song, And will magnify Him with thanksgiving.
NKJV.31: This also shall please the LORD better than an ox or bull, Which has horns and hooves.
NKJV.32: The humble shall see this and be glad; And you who seek God, your hearts shall live.
NKJV.33: For the LORD hears the poor, And does not despise His prisoners.
NKJV.34: Let heaven and earth praise Him, The seas and everything that moves in them.
NKJV.35: For God will save Zion And build the cities of Judah, That they may dwell there and possess it.
NKJV.36: Also, the descendants of His servants shall inherit it, And those who love His name shall dwell in it.

ESV.1: Save me, O God! For the waters have come up to my neck.
ESV.2: I sink in deep mire, where there is no foothold; I have come into deep waters, and the flood sweeps over me.
ESV.3: I am weary with my crying out; my throat is parched. My eyes grow dim with waiting for my God.
ESV.4: More in number than the hairs of my head are those who hate me without cause; mighty are those who would destroy me, those who attack me with lies. What I did not steal must I now restore?
ESV.5: O God, you know my folly; the wrongs I have done are not hidden from you.
ESV.6: Let not those who hope in you be put to shame through me, O Lord GOD of hosts; let not those who seek you be brought to dishonor through me, O God of Israel.
ESV.7: For it is for your sake that I have borne reproach, that dishonor has covered my face.
ESV.8: I have become a stranger to my brothers, an alien to my mother's sons.
ESV.9: For zeal for your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen on me.
ESV.10: When I wept and humbled  my soul with fasting, it became my reproach.
ESV.11: When I made sackcloth my clothing, I became a byword to them.
ESV.12: I am the talk of those who sit in the gate, and the drunkards make songs about me.
ESV.13: But as for me, my prayer is to you, O LORD. At an acceptable time, O God, in the abundance of your steadfast love answer me in your saving faithfulness.
ESV.14: Deliver me from sinking in the mire; let me be delivered from my enemies and from the deep waters.
ESV.15: Let not the flood sweep over me, or the deep swallow me up, or the pit close its mouth over me.
ESV.16: Answer me, O LORD, for your steadfast love is good; according to your abundant mercy, turn to me.
ESV.17: Hide not your face from your servant, for I am in distress; make haste to answer me.
ESV.18: Draw near to my soul, redeem me; ransom me because of my enemies!
ESV.19: You know my reproach, and my shame and my dishonor; my foes are all known to you.
ESV.20: Reproaches have broken my heart, so that I am in despair. I looked for pity, but there was none, and for comforters, but I found none.
ESV.21: They gave me poison for food, and for my thirst they gave me sour wine to drink.
ESV.22: Let their own table before them become a snare; and when they are at peace, let it become a trap.
ESV.23: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they cannot see, and make their loins tremble continually.
ESV.24: Pour out your indignation upon them, and let your burning anger overtake them.
ESV.25: May their camp be a desolation; let no one dwell in their tents.
ESV.26: For they persecute him whom you have struck down, and they recount the pain of those you have wounded.
ESV.27: Add to them punishment upon punishment; may they have no acquittal from you.
ESV.28: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living; let them not be enrolled among the righteous.
ESV.29: But I am afflicted and in pain; let your salvation, O God, set me on high!
ESV.30: I will praise the name of God with a song; I will magnify him with thanksgiving.
ESV.31: This will please the LORD more than an ox or a bull with horns and hoofs.
ESV.32: When the humble see it they will be glad; you who seek God, let your hearts revive.
ESV.33: For the LORD hears the needy and does not despise his own people who are prisoners.
ESV.34: Let heaven and earth praise him, the seas and everything that moves in them.
ESV.35: For God will save Zion and build up the cities of Judah, and people shall dwell there and possess it;
ESV.36: the offspring of his servants shall inherit it, and those who love his name shall dwell in it.

NASB.1: For the choir director; according to Shoshannim. [A Psalm] of David. Save me, O God, For the waters have threatened my life.
NASB.2: I have sunk in deep mire, and there is no foothold; I have come into deep waters, and a flood overflows me.
NASB.3: I am weary with my crying; my throat is parched; My eyes fail while I wait for my God.
NASB.4: Those who hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head; Those who would destroy me are powerful, being wrongfully my enemies; What I did not steal, I then have to restore.
NASB.5: O God, it is You who knows my folly, And my wrongs are not hidden from You.
NASB.6: May those who wait for You not be ashamed through me, O Lord GOD of hosts; May those who seek You not be dishonored through me, O God of Israel,
NASB.7: Because for Your sake I have borne reproach; Dishonor has covered my face.
NASB.8: I have become estranged from my brothers And an alien to my mother's sons.
NASB.9: For zeal for Your house has consumed me, And the reproaches of those who reproach You have fallen on me.
NASB.10: When I wept in my soul with fasting, It became my reproach.
NASB.11: When I made sackcloth my clothing, I became a byword to them.
NASB.12: Those who sit in the gate talk about me, And I [am] the song of the drunkards.
NASB.13: But as for me, my prayer is to You, O LORD, at an acceptable time; O God, in the greatness of Your lovingkindness, Answer me with Your saving truth.
NASB.14: Deliver me from the mire and do not let me sink; May I be delivered from my foes and from the deep waters.
NASB.15: May the flood of water not overflow me Nor the deep swallow me up, Nor the pit shut its mouth on me.
NASB.16: Answer me, O LORD, for Your lovingkindness is good; According to the greatness of Your compassion, turn to me,
NASB.17: And do not hide Your face from Your servant, For I am in distress; answer me quickly.
NASB.18: Oh draw near to my soul [and] redeem it; Ransom me because of my enemies!
NASB.19: You know my reproach and my shame and my dishonor; All my adversaries are before You.
NASB.20: Reproach has broken my heart and I am so sick. And I looked for sympathy, but there was none, And for comforters, but I found none.
NASB.21: They also gave me gall for my food And for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
NASB.22: May their table before them become a snare; And when they are in peace, [may it become] a trap.
NASB.23: May their eyes grow dim so that they cannot see, And make their loins shake continually.
NASB.24: Pour out Your indignation on them, And may Your burning anger overtake them.
NASB.25: May their camp be desolate; May none dwell in their tents.
NASB.26: For they have persecuted him whom You Yourself have smitten, And they tell of the pain of those whom You have wounded.
NASB.27: Add iniquity to their iniquity, And may they not come into Your righteousness.
NASB.28: May they be blotted out of the book of life And may they not be recorded with the righteous.
NASB.29: But I am afflicted and in pain; May Your salvation, O God, set me [securely] on high.
NASB.30: I will praise the name of God with song And magnify Him with thanksgiving.
NASB.31: And it will please the LORD better than an ox [Or] a young bull with horns and hoofs.
NASB.32: The humble have seen [it and] are glad; You who seek God, let your heart revive.
NASB.33: For the LORD hears the needy And does not despise His [who are] prisoners.
NASB.34: Let heaven and earth praise Him, The seas and everything that moves in them.
NASB.35: For God will save Zion and build the cities of Judah, That they may dwell there and possess it.
NASB.36: The descendants of His servants will inherit it, And those who love His name will dwell in it.

NLT.1:
Save me, O God,
for the floodwaters are up to my neck.
NLT.2:
Deeper and deeper I sink into the mire;
I can’t find a foothold.
I am in deep water,
and the floods overwhelm me.
NLT.3:
I am exhausted from crying for help;
my throat is parched.
My eyes are swollen with weeping,
waiting for my God to help me.
NLT.4:
Those who hate me without cause
outnumber the hairs on my head.
Many enemies try to destroy me with lies,
demanding that I give back what I didn’t steal.
NLT.5:
O God, you know how foolish I am;
my sins cannot be hidden from you.
NLT.6:
Don’t let those who trust in you be ashamed because of me,
O Sovereign LORD of Heaven’s Armies.
Don’t let me cause them to be humiliated,
O God of Israel.
NLT.7:
For I endure insults for your sake;
humiliation is written all over my face.
NLT.8:
Even my own brothers pretend they don’t know me;
they treat me like a stranger.
NLT.9:
Passion for your house has consumed me,
and the insults of those who insult you have fallen on me.
NLT.10:
When I weep and fast,
they scoff at me.
NLT.11:
When I dress in burlap to show sorrow,
they make fun of me.
NLT.12:
I am the favorite topic of town gossip,
and all the drunks sing about me.
NLT.13:
But I keep praying to you, LORD,
hoping this time you will show me favor.
In your unfailing love, O God,
answer my prayer with your sure salvation.
NLT.14:
Rescue me from the mud;
don’t let me sink any deeper!
Save me from those who hate me,
and pull me from these deep waters.
NLT.15:
Don’t let the floods overwhelm me,
or the deep waters swallow me,
or the pit of death devour me.
NLT.16:
Answer my prayers, O LORD,
for your unfailing love is wonderful.
Take care of me,
for your mercy is so plentiful.
NLT.17:
Don’t hide from your servant;
answer me quickly, for I am in deep trouble!
NLT.18:
Come and redeem me;
free me from my enemies.
NLT.19:
You know of my shame, scorn, and disgrace.
You see all that my enemies are doing.
NLT.20:
Their insults have broken my heart,
and I am in despair.
If only one person would show some pity;
if only one would turn and comfort me.
NLT.21:
But instead, they give me poison for food;
they offer me sour wine for my thirst.
NLT.22:
Let the bountiful table set before them become a snare
and their prosperity become a trap.
NLT.23:
Let their eyes go blind so they cannot see,
and make their bodies shake continually.
NLT.24:
Pour out your fury on them;
consume them with your burning anger.
NLT.25:
Let their homes become desolate
and their tents be deserted.
NLT.26:
To the one you have punished, they add insult to injury;
they add to the pain of those you have hurt.
NLT.27:
Pile their sins up high,
and don’t let them go free.
NLT.28:
Erase their names from the Book of Life;
don’t let them be counted among the righteous.
NLT.29:
I am suffering and in pain.
Rescue me, O God, by your saving power.
NLT.30:
Then I will praise God’s name with singing,
and I will honor him with thanksgiving.
NLT.31:
For this will please the LORD more than sacrificing cattle,
more than presenting a bull with its horns and hooves.
NLT.32:
The humble will see their God at work and be glad.
Let all who seek God’s help be encouraged.
NLT.33:
For the LORD hears the cries of the needy;
he does not despise his imprisoned people.
NLT.34:
Praise him, O heaven and earth,
the seas and all that move in them.
NLT.35:
For God will save Jerusalem
and rebuild the towns of Judah.
His people will live there
and settle in their own land.
NLT.36:
The descendants of those who obey him will inherit the land,
and those who love him will live there in safety.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Lilies.” By David. Save me, God, for the waters have come up to my neck!
WEB.2: I sink in deep mire, where there is no foothold. I have come into deep waters, where the floods overflow me.
WEB.3: I am weary with my crying. My throat is dry. My eyes fail, looking for my God.
WEB.4: Those who hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head. Those who want to cut me off, being my enemies wrongfully, are mighty. I have to restore what I didn’t take away.
WEB.5: God, you know my foolishness. My sins aren’t hidden from you.
WEB.6: Don’t let those who wait for you be shamed through me, Lord Yahweh of Armies. Don’t let those who seek you be brought to dishonor through me, God of Israel.
WEB.7: Because for your sake, I have borne reproach. Shame has covered my face.
WEB.8: I have become a stranger to my brothers, an alien to my mother’s children.
WEB.9: For the zeal of your house consumes me. The reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen on me.
WEB.10: When I wept and I fasted, that was to my reproach.
WEB.11: When I made sackcloth my clothing, I became a byword to them.
WEB.12: Those who sit in the gate talk about me. I am the song of the drunkards.
WEB.13: But as for me, my prayer is to you, Yahweh, in an acceptable time. God, in the abundance of your loving kindness, answer me in the truth of your salvation.
WEB.14: Deliver me out of the mire, and don’t let me sink. Let me be delivered from those who hate me, and out of the deep waters.
WEB.15: Don’t let the flood waters overwhelm me, neither let the deep swallow me up. Don’t let the pit shut its mouth on me.
WEB.16: Answer me, Yahweh, for your loving kindness is good. According to the multitude of your tender mercies, turn to me.
WEB.17: Don’t hide your face from your servant, for I am in distress. Answer me speedily!
WEB.18: Draw near to my soul, and redeem it. Ransom me because of my enemies.
WEB.19: You know my reproach, my shame, and my dishonor. My adversaries are all before you.
WEB.20: Reproach has broken my heart, and I am full of heaviness. I looked for some to take pity, but there was none; for comforters, but I found none.
WEB.21: They also gave me gall for my food. In my thirst, they gave me vinegar to drink.
WEB.22: Let their table before them become a snare. May it become a retribution and a trap.
WEB.23: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they can’t see. Let their backs be continually bent.
WEB.24: Pour out your indignation on them. Let the fierceness of your anger overtake them.
WEB.25: Let their habitation be desolate. Let no one dwell in their tents.
WEB.26: For they persecute him whom you have wounded. They tell of the sorrow of those whom you have hurt.
WEB.27: Charge them with crime upon crime. Don’t let them come into your righteousness.
WEB.28: Let them be blotted out of the book of life, and not be written with the righteous.
WEB.29: But I am in pain and distress. Let your salvation, God, protect me.
WEB.30: I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.
WEB.31: It will please Yahweh better than an ox, or a bull that has horns and hoofs.
WEB.32: The humble have seen it, and are glad. You who seek after God, let your heart live.
WEB.33: For Yahweh hears the needy, and doesn’t despise his captive people.
WEB.34: Let heaven and earth praise him; the seas, and everything that moves therein!
WEB.35: For God will save Zion, and build the cities of Judah. They shall settle there, and own it.
WEB.36: The children also of his servants shall inherit it. Those who love his name shall dwell therein.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: according to “The Lilies.” ⓔ Of David. [1] Save me, God,
for the water has risen to my neck. ⓕ
CSB17.2:
I have sunk in deep mud, and there is no footing;
I have come into deep water,
and a flood sweeps over me. ⓖ
CSB17.3:
I am weary from my crying;
my throat is parched.
My eyes fail, looking for my God. ⓗ
CSB17.4:
Those who hate me without cause
are more numerous than the hairs of my head;
my deceitful enemies, who would destroy me,
are powerful. ⓘ
Though I did not steal, I must repay. ⓙ
CSB17.5:
God, you know my foolishness,
and my guilty acts are not hidden from you. ⓚ
CSB17.6:
Do not let those who put their hope in you
be disgraced because of me,
Lord GOD of Armies;
do not let those who seek you
be humiliated because of me,
God of Israel. ⓛ
CSB17.7:
For I have endured insults because of you,
and shame has covered my face. ⓜ
CSB17.8:
I have become a stranger to my brothers
and a foreigner to my mother’s sons ⓝ
CSB17.9:
because zeal for your house has consumed me, ⓐ
and the insults of those who insult you
have fallen on me. ⓑ
CSB17.10:
I mourned and fasted,
but it brought me insults. ⓒ
CSB17.11:
I wore sackcloth as my clothing,
and I was a joke to them.
CSB17.12:
Those who sit at the city gate talk about me,
and drunkards make up songs about me. ⓓ
CSB17.13:
But as for me, LORD,
my prayer to you is for a time of favor.
In your abundant, faithful love, God,
answer me with your sure salvation. ⓔ
CSB17.14:
Rescue me from the miry mud; don’t let me sink.
Let me be rescued from those who hate me
and from the deep water. ⓕ
CSB17.15:
Don’t let the floodwaters sweep over me
or the deep swallow me up;
don’t let the Pit close its mouth over me. ⓖ
CSB17.16:
Answer me, LORD,
for your faithful love is good.
In keeping with your abundant compassion,
turn to me. ⓗ
CSB17.17:
Don’t hide your face from your servant,
for I am in distress.
Answer me quickly! ⓘ
CSB17.18:
Come near to me and redeem me;
ransom me because of my enemies. ⓙ
CSB17.19:
You know the insults I endure —
my shame and disgrace.
You are aware of all my adversaries. ⓚ
CSB17.20:
Insults have broken my heart,
and I am in despair.
I waited for sympathy,
but there was none;
for comforters, but found no one. ⓛ
CSB17.21:
Instead, they gave me gall for my food,
and for my thirst
they gave me vinegar to drink. ⓜ
CSB17.22:
Let their table set before them be a snare,
and let it be a trap for their allies. ⓝ
CSB17.23:
Let their eyes grow too dim to see,
and let their hips continually quake. ⓐ
CSB17.24:
Pour out your rage on them,
and let your burning anger overtake them. ⓑ
CSB17.25:
Make their fortification desolate;
may no one live in their tents. ⓒ
CSB17.26:
For they persecute the one you struck
and talk about the pain of those you wounded. ⓓ
CSB17.27:
Charge them with crime on top of crime;
do not let them share in your righteousness. ⓔ
CSB17.28:
Let them be erased from the book of life
and not be recorded with the righteous. ⓕ
CSB17.29:
But as for me — poor and in pain —
let your salvation protect me, God. ⓖ
CSB17.30:
I will praise God’s name with song
and exalt him with thanksgiving. ⓗ
CSB17.31:
That will please the LORD more than an ox,
more than a bull with horns and hooves. ⓘ
CSB17.32:
The humble will see it and rejoice.
You who seek God, take heart! ⓙ
CSB17.33:
For the LORD listens to the needy
and does not despise
his own who are prisoners. ⓚ
CSB17.34:
Let heaven and earth praise him,
the seas and everything that moves in them, ⓛ
CSB17.35:
for God will save Zion
and build up [1] the cities of Judah.
They will live there and possess it. ⓜ
CSB17.36:
The descendants of his servants will inherit it,
and those who love his name will live in it. ⓝ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to [the tune of] "Lilies." A Psalm of David. Save me, O God,
For the waters have threatened my life [they have come up to my neck].
AMP.2:
I have sunk in deep mire, where there is no foothold;
I have come into deep waters, where a flood overwhelms me.
AMP.3:
I am weary with my crying; my throat is parched;
My eyes fail while I wait [with confident expectation] for my God.
AMP.4:
Those who hate me without cause are more than the hairs of my head;
Those who would destroy me are powerful, being my enemies wrongfully;
I am forced to restore what I did not steal.
AMP.5:
O God, You know my folly;
My wrongs are not hidden from You.
AMP.6:
Do not let those who wait [confidently] for You be ashamed through me, O Lord GOD of hosts;
Do not let those who seek You [as necessary for life itself] be dishonored through me, O God of Israel,
AMP.7:
Because for Your sake I have borne reproach;
Confusion and dishonor have covered my face.
AMP.8:
I have become estranged from my brothers
And an alien to my mother's sons.
AMP.9:
For zeal for Your house has consumed me,
And the [mocking] insults of those who insult You have fallen on me.
AMP.10:
When I wept and humbled myself with fasting,
It became my reproach.
AMP.11:
When I made sackcloth my clothing [as one in mourning],
I became a byword [a mere object of scorn] to them.
AMP.12:
They who sit in the [city's] gate talk about me and mock me,
And I am the song of the drunkards.
AMP.13:
But as for me, my prayer is to You, O LORD, at an acceptable and opportune time;
O God, in the greatness of Your favor and in the abundance of Your lovingkindness,
Answer me with truth [that is, the faithfulness of Your salvation].
AMP.14:
Rescue me from the mire and do not let me sink;
Let me be rescued from those who hate me and from the deep waters.
AMP.15:
Do not let the floodwater overwhelm me,
Nor the deep waters swallow me up,
Nor the pit [of Sheol] shut its mouth over me.
AMP.16:
Answer me, O LORD, for Your lovingkindness is sweet and good and comforting;
According to the greatness of Your compassion, turn to me.
AMP.17:
Do not hide Your face from Your servant,
For I am in distress; answer me quickly.
AMP.18:
Draw near to my soul and redeem it;
Ransom me because of my enemies [so that they do not delight in my distress].
AMP.19:
You know my reproach and my shame and my dishonor [how I am insulted];
My adversaries are all before You [each one fully known].
AMP.20:
Reproach and insults have broken my heart and I am so sick.
I looked for sympathy, but there was none,
And for comforters, but I found none.
AMP.21:
They (self-righteous hypocrites) also gave me gall [poisonous and bitter] for my food,
And for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
AMP.22:
May their table [with all its abundance and luxury] become a snare [to them];
And when they are in peace [secure at their sacrificial feasts], may it become a trap.
AMP.23:
May their eyes be dimmed so that they cannot see,
And make their loins shake continually [in terror and weakness].
AMP.24:
Pour out Your indignation on them,
And let [the fierceness of] Your burning anger overtake them.
AMP.25:
May their encampment be desolate;
May no one dwell in their tents.
AMP.26:
For they have persecuted him whom You have struck,
And they tell of the pain of those whom You have pierced and wounded.
AMP.27:
Add [unforgiven] iniquity to their iniquity [in Your book],
And may they not come into Your righteousness.
AMP.28:
May they be blotted out of the book of life [and their lives come to an end]
And may they not be recorded with the righteous (those in right standing with God).
AMP.29:
But I am sorrowful and in pain;
May Your salvation, O God, set me [securely] on high.
AMP.30:
I will praise the name of God with song
And magnify Him with thanksgiving.
AMP.31:
And it will please the LORD better than an ox
Or a young bull with horns and hoofs.
AMP.32:
The humble have seen it and are glad;
You who seek God [requiring Him as your greatest need], let your heart revive and live.
AMP.33:
For the LORD hears the needy
And does not despise His who are prisoners.
AMP.34:
Let heaven and earth praise Him,
The seas and everything that moves in them.
AMP.35:
For God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah,
That His servants may remain there and possess it.
AMP.36:
The descendants of His servants will inherit it,
And those who love His name will dwell in it.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God, God, save me!
I'm in over my head,
MSG.2:
Quicksand under me, swamp water over me;
I'm going down for the third time.
MSG.3:
I'm hoarse from calling for help,
Bleary-eyed from searching the sky for God.
MSG.4:
I've got more enemies than hairs on my head;
Sneaks and liars are out to knife me in the back.
What I never stole
Must I now give back?
MSG.5:
God, you know every sin I've committed;
My life's a wide-open book before you.
MSG.6:
Don't let those who look to you in hope
Be discouraged by what happens to me,
Dear Lord! God of the armies!
Don't let those out looking for you
Come to a dead end by following me—
Please, dear God of Israel!
MSG.7:
Because of you I look like an idiot,
I walk around ashamed to show my face.
MSG.8:
My brothers shun me like a bum off the street;
My family treats me like an unwanted guest.
MSG.9:
I love you more than I can say.
Because I'm madly in love with you,
They blame me for everything they dislike about you.
MSG.10:
When I poured myself out in prayer and fasting,
All it got me was more contempt.
MSG.11:
When I put on a sad face,
They treated me like a clown.
MSG.12:
Now drunks and gluttons
Make up drinking songs about me.
MSG.13:
And me? I pray.
God, it's time for a break!
God, answer in love!
Answer with your sure salvation!
MSG.14:
Rescue me from the swamp,
Don't let me go under for good,
Pull me out of the clutch of the enemy;
This whirlpool is sucking me down.
MSG.15:
Don't let the swamp be my grave, the Black Hole
Swallow me, its jaws clenched around me.
MSG.16:
Now answer me, God, because you love me;
Let me see your great mercy full-face.
MSG.17:
Don't look the other way; your servant can't take it.
I'm in trouble. Answer right now!
MSG.18:
Come close, God; get me out of here.
Rescue me from this deathtrap.
MSG.19:
You know how they kick me around—
Pin on me the donkey's ears, the dunce's cap.
MSG.20:
I'm broken by their taunts,
Flat on my face, reduced to a nothing.
I looked in vain for one friendly face. Not one.
I couldn't find one shoulder to cry on.
MSG.21:
They put poison in my soup,
Vinegar in my drink.
MSG.22:
Let their supper be bait in a trap that snaps shut;
May their best friends be trappers who'll skin them alive.
MSG.23:
Make them become blind as bats,
Give them the shakes from morning to night.
MSG.24:
Let them know what you think of them,
Blast them with your red-hot anger.
MSG.25:
Burn down their houses,
Leave them desolate with nobody at home.
MSG.26:
They gossiped about the one you disciplined,
Made up stories about anyone wounded by God.
MSG.27:
Pile on the guilt,
Don't let them off the hook.
MSG.28:
Strike their names from the list of the living;
No rock-carved honor for them among the righteous.
MSG.29:
I'm hurt and in pain;
Give me space for healing, and mountain air.
MSG.30:
Let me shout God's name with a praising song,
Let me tell his greatness in a prayer of thanks.
MSG.31:
For God, this is better than oxen on the altar,
Far better than blue-ribbon bulls.
MSG.32:
The poor in spirit see and are glad—
Oh, you God-seekers, take heart!
MSG.33:
For God listens to the poor,
He doesn't walk out on the wretched.
MSG.34:
You heavens, praise him; praise him, earth;
Also ocean and all things that swim in it.
MSG.35:
For God is out to help Zion,
Rebuilding the wrecked towns of Judah.
Guess who will live there—
The proud owners of the land?
MSG.36:
No, the children of his servants will get it,
The lovers of his name will live in it.

NIV.1:
Psalm 69
For the director of music. To the tune of “Lilies.” Of David.
Save me, O God,
for the waters have come up to my neck.
NIV.2:
I sink in the miry depths,
where there is no foothold.
I have come into the deep waters;
the floods engulf me.
NIV.3:
I am worn out calling for help;
my throat is parched.
My eyes fail,
looking for my God.
NIV.4:
Those who hate me without reason
outnumber the hairs of my head;
many are my enemies without cause,
those who seek to destroy me.
I am forced to restore
what I did not steal.
NIV.5:
You know my folly, O God;
my guilt is not hidden from you.
NIV.6:
May those who hope in you
not be disgraced because of me,
O Lord, the Lord Almighty;
may those who seek you
not be put to shame because of me,
O God of Israel.
NIV.7:
For I endure scorn for your sake,
and shame covers my face.
NIV.8:
I am a stranger to my brothers,
an alien to my own mother’s sons;
NIV.9:
for zeal for your house consumes me,
and the insults of those who insult you fall on me.
NIV.10:
When I weep and fast,
I must endure scorn;
NIV.11:
when I put on sackcloth,
people make sport of me.
NIV.12:
Those who sit at the gate mock me,
and I am the song of the drunkards.
NIV.13:
But I pray to you, O Lord,
in the time of your favor;
in your great love, O God,
answer me with your sure salvation.
NIV.14:
Rescue me from the mire,
do not let me sink;
deliver me from those who hate me,
from the deep waters.
NIV.15:
Do not let the floodwaters engulf me
or the depths swallow me up
or the pit close its mouth over me.
NIV.16:
Answer me, O Lord, out of the goodness of your love;
in your great mercy turn to me.
NIV.17:
Do not hide your face from your servant;
answer me quickly, for I am in trouble.
NIV.18:
Come near and rescue me;
redeem me because of my foes.
NIV.19:
You know how I am scorned, disgraced and shamed;
all my enemies are before you.
NIV.20:
Scorn has broken my heart
and has left me helpless;
I looked for sympathy, but there was none,
for comforters, but I found none.
NIV.21:
They put gall in my food
and gave me vinegar for my thirst.
NIV.22:
May the table set before them become a snare;
may it become retribution and a trap.
NIV.23:
May their eyes be darkened so they cannot see,
and their backs be bent forever.
NIV.24:
Pour out your wrath on them;
let your fierce anger overtake them.
NIV.25:
May their place be deserted;
let there be no one to dwell in their tents.
NIV.26:
For they persecute those you wound
and talk about the pain of those you hurt.
NIV.27:
Charge them with crime upon crime;
do not let them share in your salvation.
NIV.28:
May they be blotted out of the book of life
and not be listed with the righteous.
NIV.29:
I am in pain and distress;
may your salvation, O God, protect me.
NIV.30:
I will praise God’s name in song
and glorify him with thanksgiving.
NIV.31:
This will please the Lord more than an ox,
more than a bull with its horns and hoofs.
NIV.32:
The poor will see and be glad—
you who seek God, may your hearts live!
NIV.33:
The Lord hears the needy
and does not despise his captive people.
NIV.34:
Let heaven and earth praise him,
the seas and all that move in them,
NIV.35:
for God will save Zion
and rebuild the cities of Judah.
Then people will settle there and possess it;
NIV.36:
the children of his servants will inherit it,
and those who love his name will dwell there.

NRSVCE.1:
Save me, O God,
for the waters have come up to my neck.
NRSVCE.2:
I sink in deep mire,
where there is no foothold;
I have come into deep waters,
and the flood sweeps over me.
NRSVCE.3:
I am weary with my crying;
my throat is parched.
My eyes grow dim
with waiting for my God.
NRSVCE.4:
More in number than the hairs of my head
are those who hate me without cause;
many are those who would destroy me,
my enemies who accuse me falsely.
What I did not steal
must I now restore?
NRSVCE.5:
O God, you know my folly;
the wrongs I have done are not hidden from you.
NRSVCE.6:
Do not let those who hope in you be put to shame because of me,
O Lord GOD of hosts;
do not let those who seek you be dishonored because of me,
O God of Israel.
NRSVCE.7:
It is for your sake that I have borne reproach,
that shame has covered my face.
NRSVCE.8:
I have become a stranger to my kindred,
an alien to my mother’s children.
NRSVCE.9:
It is zeal for your house that has consumed me;
the insults of those who insult you have fallen on me.
NRSVCE.10:
When I humbled my soul with fasting,
they insulted me for doing so.
NRSVCE.11:
When I made sackcloth my clothing,
I became a byword to them.
NRSVCE.12:
I am the subject of gossip for those who sit in the gate,
and the drunkards make songs about me.
NRSVCE.13:
But as for me, my prayer is to you, O LORD.
At an acceptable time, O God,
in the abundance of your steadfast love, answer me.
With your faithful help
NRSVCE.14:
rescue me
from sinking in the mire;
let me be delivered from my enemies
and from the deep waters.
NRSVCE.15:
Do not let the flood sweep over me,
or the deep swallow me up,
or the Pit close its mouth over me.
NRSVCE.16:
Answer me, O LORD, for your steadfast love is good;
according to your abundant mercy, turn to me.
NRSVCE.17:
Do not hide your face from your servant,
for I am in distress — make haste to answer me.
NRSVCE.18:
Draw near to me, redeem me,
set me free because of my enemies.
NRSVCE.19:
You know the insults I receive,
and my shame and dishonor;
my foes are all known to you.
NRSVCE.20:
Insults have broken my heart,
so that I am in despair.
I looked for pity, but there was none;
and for comforters, but I found none.
NRSVCE.21:
They gave me poison for food,
and for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
NRSVCE.22:
Let their table be a trap for them,
a snare for their allies.
NRSVCE.23:
Let their eyes be darkened so that they cannot see,
and make their loins tremble continually.
NRSVCE.24:
Pour out your indignation upon them,
and let your burning anger overtake them.
NRSVCE.25:
May their camp be a desolation;
let no one live in their tents.
NRSVCE.26:
For they persecute those whom you have struck down,
and those whom you have wounded, they attack still more.
NRSVCE.27:
Add guilt to their guilt;
may they have no acquittal from you.
NRSVCE.28:
Let them be blotted out of the book of the living;
let them not be enrolled among the righteous.
NRSVCE.29:
But I am lowly and in pain;
let your salvation, O God, protect me.
NRSVCE.30:
I will praise the name of God with a song;
I will magnify him with thanksgiving.
NRSVCE.31:
This will please the LORD more than an ox
or a bull with horns and hoofs.
NRSVCE.32:
Let the oppressed see it and be glad;
you who seek God, let your hearts revive.
NRSVCE.33:
For the LORD hears the needy,
and does not despise his own that are in bonds.
NRSVCE.34:
Let heaven and earth praise him,
the seas and everything that moves in them.
NRSVCE.35:
For God will save Zion
and rebuild the cities of Judah;
and his servants shall live there and possess it;
NRSVCE.36:
the children of his servants shall inherit it,
and those who love his name shall live in it.

NET.1: Deliver me, O God, for the water has reached my neck.
NET.2: I sink into the deep mire where there is no solid ground; I am in deep water, and the current overpowers me.
NET.3: I am exhausted from shouting for help; my throat is sore; my eyes grow tired of looking for my God.
NET.4: Those who hate me without cause are more numerous than the hairs of my head. Those who want to destroy me, my enemies for no reason, outnumber me. They make me repay what I did not steal!
NET.5: O God, you are aware of my foolish sins; my guilt is not hidden from you.
NET.6: Let none who rely on you be disgraced because of me, O sovereign LORD and king! Let none who seek you be ashamed because of me, O God of Israel!
NET.7: For I suffer humiliation for your sake and am thoroughly disgraced.
NET.8: My own brothers treat me like a stranger; they act as if I were a foreigner.
NET.9: Certainly zeal for your house consumes me; I endure the insults of those who insult you.
NET.10: I weep and refrain from eating food, which causes others to insult me.
NET.11: I wear sackcloth and they ridicule me.
NET.12: Those who sit at the city gate gossip about me; drunkards mock me in their songs.
NET.13: O LORD, may you hear my prayer and be favorably disposed to me! O God, because of your great loyal love, answer me with your faithful deliverance!
NET.14: Rescue me from the mud! Don’t let me sink! Deliver me from those who hate me, from the deep water!
NET.15: Don’t let the current overpower me! Don’t let the deep swallow me up! Don’t let the pit devour me!
NET.16: Answer me, O LORD, for your loyal love is good! Because of your great compassion, turn toward me!
NET.17: Do not ignore your servant, for I am in trouble! Answer me right away!
NET.18: Come near me and redeem me! Because of my enemies, rescue me!
NET.19: You know how I am insulted, humiliated and disgraced; you can see all my enemies.
NET.20: Their insults are painful and make me lose heart; I look for sympathy, but receive none, for comforters, but find none.
NET.21: They put bitter poison into my food, and to quench my thirst they give me vinegar to drink.
NET.22: May their dining table become a trap before them! May it be a snare for that group of friends!
NET.23: May their eyes be blinded! Make them shake violently!
NET.24: Pour out your judgment on them! May your raging anger overtake them!
NET.25: May their camp become desolate, their tents uninhabited!
NET.26: For they harass the one whom you discipline; they spread the news about the suffering of those whom you punish.
NET.27: Hold them accountable for all their sins! Do not vindicate them!
NET.28: May their names be deleted from the scroll of the living! Do not let their names be listed with the godly!
NET.29: I am oppressed and suffering! O God, deliver and protect me!
NET.30: I will sing praises to God’s name! I will magnify him as I give him thanks!
NET.31: That will please the LORD more than an ox or a bull with horns and hooves.
NET.32: The oppressed look on — let them rejoice! You who seek God, may you be encouraged!
NET.33: For the LORD listens to the needy; he does not despise his captive people.
NET.34: Let the heavens and the earth praise him, along with the seas and everything that swims in them!
NET.35: For God will deliver Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah, and his people will again live in them and possess Zion.
NET.36: The descendants of his servants will inherit it, and those who are loyal to him will live in it.
